analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 3 finding(s) from 8 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Debug/JTAG and debug NoC request paths appear reachable without an RTL use-site gate on jtag_unlock or acc_ctrl_c before dm_top debug registers and hart debug/reset controls.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMI read/write and hart debug/reset control via dm_top; NoC AXI-lite access to debug module slave interface / dm_top debug registers, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave interface; downstream dm_master system-bus access is tied off / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: External JTAG DTM / simulation DTM requester and NoC debug requester Operation: DMI read/write and hart debug/reset control via dm_top; NoC AXI-lite access to debug module slave interface Object: dm_top debug registers, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave interface; downstream dm_master system-bus access is tied off Expected guard: jtag_unlock or equivalent lifecycle/debug authorization should mediate DMI requests and NoC debug access before debug registers or hart controls can be reached Observed behavior: dmi_jtag produces jtag_unlock_o, but visible RTL evidence shows DMI request/valid signals connected onward to dm_top without a jtag_unlock gate at the use site. Separately, the debug NoC bridge feeds dm_master AXI, axi2mem, and dm_slave_* into the debug module path with no visible acc_ctrl_c or jtag_unlock gate in riscv_peripherals. dm_master system-bus response/grant is tied off, limiting downstream system-bus memory access, but debug module slave registers and hart controls remain reachable. Path: tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or SimDTM -> dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> debug_req_valid/debug_req -> dm_top -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o; buf_ariane_debug_noc2_* -> i_debug_axilite_bridge -> dm_master AXI -> i_dm_axi2mem -> dm_slave_* -> dm_top Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An unmediated DMI or debug NoC path can access debug registers and assert hart debug request/reset controls, enabling privileged debug entry or reset effects independent of normal software privilege/access-control policy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Exact dm_top instantiation lines are partially supplied by observation/model summary, not fully shown in raw snippets. Authorization behavior inside dmi_jtag and dm_top is not inspected.

findings.summary: reglk_wrapper clears lock memory when jtag_unlock is asserted, so an unlock signal can reset lock bits without a visible sticky/lifecycle guard at the wrapper.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/set/clear/unlock lock-control bits / reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_mem lock bits controlling peripheral/register write protection / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI software requester and JTAG unlock path Operation: write/set/clear/unlock lock-control bits Object: reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_mem lock bits controlling peripheral/register write protection Expected guard: lock registers should be writable only under allowed conditions, become sticky or clear only under authorized reset/unlock, and guard protected write paths at use sites Observed behavior: reglk_wrapper gates local AXI acceptance by acct_ctrl_i, and writes to reglk_mem are blocked by selected reglk_ctrl_i bits. However, reset logic uses if (~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9)) to clear all reglk_mem entries to zero, meaning assertion of jtag_unlock clears lock state. The top-level source of jtag_unlock is dmi_jtag, and no additional authorization/stickiness is visible in the reglk_wrapper reset path. Path: AXI writes -> axi_lite_interface -> en_acct/we/address/wdata -> reglk_wrapper reglk_mem -> reglk_ctrl_o; dmi_jtag jtag_unlock -> reglk_wrapper reset condition -> reglk_mem cleared Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If jtag_unlock can be asserted by an attacker or unintended debug/test condition, established lock protections can be cleared, permitting later modification of protected control registers and weakening peripheral write protection.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Whether dmi_jtag authentication is cryptographically sufficient is not inspected; nevertheless, wrapper behavior shows jtag_unlock is a lock-clear input.

findings.summary: CLINT NoC AXI-lite path is connected directly without visible acc_ctrl_c/privilege gating at the top level or in axi_lite_interface.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write CLINT registers / CLINT timer/IPI control registers accessed through axi_lite_interface/noc_axilite_bridge / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI/NoC requester Operation: read/write CLINT registers Object: CLINT timer/IPI control registers accessed through axi_lite_interface/noc_axilite_bridge Expected guard: writes to timer compare/software interrupt or other CLINT control state should be allowed only for authorized privilege/access-control context or protected by upstream peripheral gate Observed behavior: The CLINT endpoint is connected from NoC bridge signals directly into clint_axi_req/resp and then into the clint instance. The supplied CLINT bridge/top-level snippets do not show acct_ctrl_i, acc_ctrl_c, reglk_ctrl, or privilege gating. The generic axi_lite_interface itself produces en_o/we_o from handshakes and returns OK responses with no local authorization checks. Path: buf_ariane_clint_noc2_* -> i_clint_axilite_bridge -> clint_axi_req.aw/ar/w -> i_clint -> timer_irq_o/ipi_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A NoC requester that can reach the CLINT endpoint could write timer/IPI state or read CLINT registers without the access-control matrix enforced at this boundary, potentially causing unauthorized interrupts or timer manipulation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Internal clint module authorization logic is not provided; the finding is based on absence of visible upstream gate in provided top-level and generic AXI-lite interface evidence.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
